name: R-parity-violating SUSY
summary: Drops the R-parity assumption from MSSM, allowing renormalisable lepton-number- and baryon-number-violating couplings W = lambda L L E + lambda' L Q D + lambda'' U D D + mu L H_u. Permits the LSP to decay (so it's no longer a DM candidate), enables fast proton decay unless one of B or L violation is forbidden, and produces distinctive collider signatures.

addresses_puzzles.note: Bilinear R-parity-violating term mu_i L_i H_u generates Majorana neutrino masses radiatively at tree- and loop-level without an explicit seesaw; predicts specific PMNS structure tied to the sneutrino-VEV pattern.

touches_benchmarks.note: Both B-violating and L-violating RPV couplings generate p -> e+ pi^0 / p -> K nu unless one is forbidden by symmetry; combination of lambda' lambda'' < 10^-25 required to be safe.
Trilinear lambda couplings generate mu -> e gamma at tree level; current MEG bound BR < 4.2e-13 constrains lambda_ijk < 10^-3 for slepton masses ~ 100 GeV.
Neutrino Majorana masses from RPV bilinear contribute to 0nubb at experimentally relevant rates.

new_symmetries: broken R-parity (or surviving baryon triality / lepton triality)
introduces.note: Builds on MSSM. Adds 9 trilinear lambda + 27 lambda' + 9 lambda'' + 3 bilinear mu_i, plus their soft-breaking partners — the most parameter-heavy SUSY extension.

history: Hall and Suzuki (1984), 'Explicit R-parity breaking in supersymmetric models', Nucl. Phys. B 231, 419. Aulakh-Mohapatra spontaneous R-parity violation (1982). Distinguishing collider signatures (multi-leptons, displaced vertices) made RPV a focus of LHC searches; current bounds are stringent but not closed.

references.citation: Hall and Suzuki, 'Explicit R-parity breaking in supersymmetric models', Nucl. Phys. B 231, 419 (1984)
